featured.title: Summer Harvest Buddha Bowl with Tahini
featured.description: A vibrant, nutrient-packed bowl featuring roasted seasonal vegetables, quinoa, chickpeas, and a creamy lemon-tahini dressing. The perfect balance of flavor and nourishment.

instructions.title: Cook the Quinoa
instructions.description: Rinse quinoa under cold water. Combine with 2 cups of water in a saucepan, bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until fluffy.

instructions.title: Roast the Sweet Potato
instructions.description: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Toss cubed sweet potato with 1 tbsp olive oil and a pinch of salt. Spread on a baking sheet and roast for 20 minutes until golden and tender.

instructions.title: Prepare the Tahini Dressing
instructions.description: Whisk together tahini, lemon juice, minced garlic, and 2-3 tbsp warm water until smooth and pourable. Season with salt to taste.

instructions.title: Assemble the Bowl
instructions.description: Divide quinoa among bowls. Arrange roasted sweet potato, chickpeas, kale, avocado slices, and cherry tomatoes on top. Drizzle generously with tahini dressing and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Mediterranean Orzo Salad
recipes.description: A refreshing mix of kalamata olives, feta, and heirloom tomatoes tossed with perfectly cooked orzo and a bright lemon-herb vinaigrette.

instructions.title: Cook the Orzo
instructions.description: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook orzo according to package directions until al dente, about 8-9 minutes. Drain, rinse under cold water, and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Vinaigrette
instructions.description: In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, dried oregano, salt, and pepper until well combined.

instructions.title: Combine and Toss
instructions.description: In a large bowl, combine the cooled orzo with olives, feta, tomatoes, cucumber, red onion, and basil. Pour the vinaigrette over the salad and toss gently to coat everything evenly.

instructions.title: Chill and Serve
instructions.description: Refrigerate for at least 15 minutes to let the flavors meld. Serve chilled or at room temperature, garnished with extra feta and basil.

recipes.title: Avocado & Poached Egg Toast
recipes.description: Perfectly sourdough base topped with crushed avocado, a silky poached egg, chili flakes, and microgreens. Simple, elevated, unforgettable.

instructions.title: Toast the Bread
instructions.description: Toast sourdough slices until golden and crisp on both sides. Set aside.

instructions.title: Poach the Eggs
instructions.description: Bring a pot of water to a gentle simmer. Add vinegar. Create a swirl in the water and carefully drop in each egg. Poach for 3-4 minutes until the whites are set but the yolks remain runny.

instructions.title: Prepare the Avocado
instructions.description: Halve the avocado and scoop out the flesh. Mash roughly with a fork, keeping some chunks for texture. Season with lemon juice and a pinch of salt.

instructions.title: Assemble
instructions.description: Spread the mashed avocado generously onto each toast. Place a poached egg on top, sprinkle with chili flakes and flaky salt, and finish with fresh microgreens.

recipes.title: Cloud-Light Buttermilk Pancakes
recipes.description: Extra fluffy pancakes made with aged buttermilk and a touch of vanilla, cooked to golden perfection. Served with maple syrup and fresh berries.

instructions.title: Mix Dry Ingredients
instructions.description: In a large bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.

instructions.title: Combine Wet Ingredients
instructions.description: In a separate bowl, whisk together buttermilk, egg, melted butter, and vanilla extract until smooth.

instructions.title: Make the Batter
instructions.description: Pour wet ingredients into dry ingredients and stir gently until just combined. A few lumps are fine — do not overmix or the pancakes will be tough.

instructions.title: Cook the Pancakes
instructions.description: Heat a non-stick pan or griddle over medium heat. Lightly butter the surface. Pour 1/4 cup of batter per pancake. Cook until bubbles form on the surface and edges look set, about 2-3 minutes, then flip and cook another 1-2 minutes until golden.

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.description: Stack pancakes on warm plates. Drizzle with maple syrup and top with fresh berries. Serve immediately while hot and fluffy.

recipes.title: Wild Mushroom Pappardelle
recipes.description: Handmade pasta tossed with sautéed forest mushrooms, thyme, garlic, and a splash of white wine cream sauce. Finished with shaved Parmigiano-Reggiano.

instructions.title: Cook the Pasta
instructions.description: Bring a large pot of heavily salted water to a rolling boil. Cook pappardelle until al dente, about 7-8 minutes. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining.

instructions.title: Sauté the Mushrooms
instructions.description: Heat olive oil and 1 tbsp butter in a large skillet over high heat. Add mushrooms in a single layer — do not crowd the pan. Cook without stirring for 3-4 minutes until deeply golden, then stir and cook another 2 minutes. Season with salt.

instructions.title: Build the Sauce
instructions.description: Reduce heat to medium. Add garlic and thyme, cook 30 seconds until fragrant. Deglaze with white wine, scraping up any brown bits. Let wine reduce by half, about 2 minutes. Add cream and remaining butter, stir until sauce thickens slightly.

instructions.title: Combine and Serve
instructions.description: Add drained pasta to the skillet and toss with the mushroom cream sauce. Add splashes of pasta water as needed for a silky consistency. Plate and top with shaved Parmigiano-Reggiano, cracked pepper, and a sprig of thyme.

recipes.title: Roasted Autumn Squash with Miso Glaze & Pepitas
recipes.description: An editorial take on seasonal comfort. The deep, earthy sweetness of roasted Kabocha squash is elevated by a savory-sweet white miso glaze, finished with the textural crunch of toasted pepitas and fresh scallions.

instructions.title: Preheat and Prep
instructions.description: Heat your oven to 425°F (220°C). While the oven reaches temperature, halve the squash, scoop out the seeds, and cut into 1-inch thick wedges. There is no need to peel Kabocha — the skin is delicious and tender when roasted.

instructions.title: Whisk the Glaze
instructions.description: In a small bowl, whisk together the miso paste, maple syrup, rice vinegar, and a splash of neutral oil until a smooth, velvety consistency forms. If it's too thick, add a teaspoon of warm water.

instructions.title: Roast to Perfection
instructions.description: Arrange squash on a baking sheet lined with parchment. Brush generously with half of the miso glaze. Roast for 20 minutes, then flip, brush with the remaining glaze, and roast for another 10-15 minutes until caramelized and tender.

instructions.title: Finishing Touches
instructions.description: Transfer the warm squash to a wide serving platter. Scatter the toasted pepitas and sliced scallions over the top. Finish with a generous pinch of flaky sea salt to brighten the miso notes.

recipes.title: Seared Salmon with Crispy Skin & Herb Butter
recipes.description: Restaurant-quality pan-seared salmon with impossibly crispy skin, topped with a fragrant compound butter of dill, parsley, and lemon zest. Served over wilted spinach.

instructions.title: Make the Herb Butter
instructions.description: Combine softened butter with dill, parsley, lemon zest, and a pinch of salt. Mix well and set aside at room temperature.

instructions.title: Prepare the Salmon
instructions.description: Pat salmon fillets completely dry with paper towels — this is essential for crispy skin. Season both sides generously with salt and pepper.

instructions.title: Sear Skin-Side Down
instructions.description: Heat olive oil in a cast iron skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering. Place salmon skin-side down and press gently with a spatula for the first 30 seconds. Cook undisturbed for 4 minutes until skin is deeply golden and crisp. Flip and cook 2 more minutes for medium.

instructions.title: Wilt the Spinach
instructions.description: In the same pan, add sliced garlic and cook 30 seconds. Add spinach, toss until just wilted, about 1 minute. Season lightly with salt.

instructions.title: Plate and Serve
instructions.description: Divide wilted spinach between plates. Place salmon on top, skin-side up to preserve the crunch. Crown each fillet with a generous dollop of herb butter and let it melt down the sides.

recipes.title: Spicy Korean Beef Bibimbap
recipes.description: A sizzling stone bowl of rice topped with marinated bulgogi beef, pickled vegetables, a perfectly fried egg, and fiery gochujang sauce.

instructions.title: Cook the Rice
instructions.description: Rinse rice until water runs clear. Cook in a rice cooker or pot with 1.75 cups water. Once done, keep covered and set aside.

instructions.title: Marinate the Beef
instructions.description: Combine sliced beef with soy sauce, 1 tbsp gochujang, sesame oil, and minced garlic. Let marinate for at least 15 minutes while you prepare the vegetables.

instructions.title: Prepare the Toppings
instructions.description: Sauté each vegetable separately: julienned carrots until tender, blanched spinach with a dash of sesame oil, and bean sprouts until just wilted. Season each lightly with salt. Set aside in separate piles.

instructions.title: Cook the Beef
instructions.description: Sear marinated beef in a hot skillet over high heat for 2-3 minutes until caramelized. Remove and set aside.

instructions.title: Assemble the Bowls
instructions.description: Divide rice into bowls. Arrange beef, vegetables, and kimchi in sections on top. Fry eggs sunny-side up and place in the center. Drizzle with remaining gochujang and sprinkle sesame seeds. Mix everything together before eating.

recipes.title: Lemon Ricotta Blueberry Pancakes
recipes.description: Impossibly tender ricotta-enriched pancakes studded with juicy blueberries and brightened with fresh lemon zest. A weekend brunch showstopper.

instructions.title: Make the Batter Base
instructions.description: Whisk egg yolks with ricotta, sugar, lemon zest, and vanilla until smooth. Fold in flour and baking powder gently — do not overmix.

instructions.title: Whip the Egg Whites
instructions.description: In a clean bowl, whip egg whites to stiff peaks using a whisk or electric mixer. This is the secret to the cloud-like texture.

instructions.title: Fold Together
instructions.description: Gently fold the whipped egg whites into the ricotta batter in two additions. The batter should be airy and billowy. Fold in blueberries at the very end.

instructions.title: Cook Low and Slow
instructions.description: Melt butter in a non-stick pan over medium-low heat. Spoon batter into rounds. Cook for 3-4 minutes until bubbles appear and the bottom is golden, then gently flip and cook 2-3 more minutes.

instructions.title: Serve Warm
instructions.description: Stack pancakes and drizzle with maple syrup. Top with extra blueberries and a dusting of powdered sugar for an elegant finish.

recipes.title: Thai Basil Chicken Stir-Fry
recipes.description: An aromatic, fiery stir-fry with tender chicken, crisp vegetables, and the unmistakable fragrance of Thai holy basil, served over jasmine rice.

instructions.title: Prep the Sauce
instructions.description: Mix fish sauce, oyster sauce, soy sauce, and sugar in a small bowl. Stir until sugar dissolves. Set aside.

instructions.title: High-Heat Sear
instructions.description: Heat oil in a wok over the highest heat until smoking. Add chicken in a single layer and sear without moving for 2 minutes until golden. Stir and cook another minute until nearly done. Remove from wok.

instructions.title: Aromatics and Vegetables
instructions.description: Add garlic and chilies to the wok, stir for 15 seconds. Add bell pepper slices and stir-fry for 1 minute until slightly charred but still crisp.

instructions.title: Bring It Together
instructions.description: Return chicken to the wok. Pour in the sauce mixture and toss everything over high heat for 30 seconds. Kill the heat, add Thai basil, and toss until just wilted. Serve immediately over steamed jasmine rice.

recipes.title: Charred Broccolini with Tahini & Dukkah
recipes.description: Smoky, blistered broccolini drizzled with creamy tahini and showered in homemade dukkah. A side dish that steals the show from any main course.

instructions.title: Make the Dukkah
instructions.description: Toast hazelnuts, sesame seeds, coriander, and cumin in a dry pan over medium heat for 2-3 minutes until fragrant. Crush roughly in a mortar and pestle. Set aside.

instructions.title: Char the Broccolini
instructions.description: Heat olive oil in a large skillet or grill pan over high heat. Add broccolini and garlic, cook without moving for 3-4 minutes until deeply charred on the bottom. Flip and char the other side for 2 minutes. Season with salt.

instructions.title: Make the Tahini Drizzle
instructions.description: Whisk tahini with lemon juice and 2 tbsp water until smooth and pourable. Adjust consistency with more water if needed.

instructions.title: Plate and Finish
instructions.description: Arrange charred broccolini on a serving platter. Drizzle generously with the tahini sauce, then shower with the dukkah. Finish with a pinch of flaky salt.
